task_completion.description: The Sugar Pippy activity is open and contains Python code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Pippy interface (a simple Python IDE in the Sugar desktop environment) with Python code visible in the editor.

task_completion.description: The Python code specifies saving the output to the correct file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The code in Pippy contains a string or file operation explicitly targeting '/home/ga/Documents/solar_system.svg'.

task_completion.description: The code or rendered SVG includes the Sun as a large yellow, orange, or gold circle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Python code constructs an SVG `<circle>` tag with a large radius and a fill color like yellow, orange, or gold for the Sun, or a rendered SVG image shows this.

task_completion.description: The code or rendered SVG includes all 8 planets with distinct colors and text labels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Python code constructs SVG `<circle>` or `<ellipse>` tags and `<text>` tags for Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune, each with different fill colors, or a rendered SVG image shows all 8 labeled planets.

task_completion.description: The planets have varying radii reflecting approximate relative sizes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The radius values ('r' attribute) in the SVG code or the rendered sizes in the image show Jupiter as the largest planet and Mercury as the smallest.

task_completion.description: The Pippy program is saved with the title 'Solar System Generator'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Pippy activity title bar (top left of the Sugar interface) shows 'Solar System Generator', or the Sugar Journal shows an entry with this exact title.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sugar Pippy activity to write the code.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code is written inside the Pippy interface, not in a terminal (like nano/vim) or a standard Linux text editor.

integrity.description: The agent generated the SVG via Python code rather than downloading or copying an existing SVG.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is visible Python code that constructs the SVG string/file, rather than terminal commands like `wget` or `curl` fetching an SVG file.

integrity.description: The program is saved in the Sugar environment's Journal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The title is set in the Sugar activity bar or visible in the Sugar Journal view, consistent with normal Sugar OS operation, rather than just renaming a file in a standard file manager.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected SVG output path: '/home/ga/Documents/solar_system.svg'. Required Sugar Journal title: 'Solar System Generator'. Required planets: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune.
